title: Instructional Goals and Objectives
description: Conroe ISD uses this policy to set clear goals for what students should learn and achieve across all grade levels and subject areas. It guides how the district plans and delivers instruction to ensure every student has access to a meaningful education. Parents and community members can look to this policy to understand the district's overall commitment to student learning and academic success.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD Board Policy EA (Local) establishes a new "Availability of Instructional Plan or Course Syllabus" section, requiring the District to post instructional plans or course syllabi on its website each semester and requiring teachers to provide copies to district administration and parents of enrolled students, with additional copies available upon parent request.
changes_added: Availability of Instructional Plan or Course Syllabus (section heading)
At the beginning of each semester, the District shall post on its website an instructional plan or course syllabus for each class offered in the District that semester.

In order for the District to make this required posting, each teacher shall provide a copy of the instructional plan or course syllabus for each class for which the teacher provides instruction to: 1. District administration; and 2. The parent of each student enrolled in the class.

Additionally, each teacher shall provide for additional copies of the instructional plan or course syllabus for each class for which the teacher provides instruction to be made available to a parent of a student enrolled in the class on the parent's request.
timeline_local.notes: The entire policy page (page 2) appears to be new/added content — all text is rendered in red underline with no black unchanged text and no red strikethrough deletions. This appears to be a brand-new local policy being adopted for the first time (Policy Update 1, as noted at the bottom of the page).

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's Board Policy EA (Local) – Instructional Goals and Objectives creates a new "Availability of Instructional Plan or Course Syllabus" section. It requires the District to post instructional plans or course syllabi on its website at the start of each semester, directs teachers to provide copies to district administration and parents, and obligates teachers to make additional copies available to parents upon request.
changes_added: Availability of Instructional Plan or Course Syllabus (section heading)
At the beginning of each semester, the District shall post on its website an instructional plan or course syllabus for each class offered in the District that semester.

In order for the District to make this required posting, each teacher shall provide a copy of the instructional plan or course syllabus for each class for which the teacher provides instruction to: 1. District administration; and 2. The parent of each student enrolled in the class.

Additionally, each teacher shall provide for additional copies of the instructional plan or course syllabus for each class for which the teacher provides instruction to be made available to a parent of a student enrolled in the class on the parent's request.
timeline_local.notes: Page 2 shows the entire policy body in red underlined text, indicating this is entirely new language being added to a previously blank or minimal local policy. There is no red strikethrough text, so no language is being removed. The cover page heading reads "Discuss Revisions to Board Policy EA (Local)," indicating a first reading.

description_local: This policy establishes requirements for how Conroe ISD teachers document and share their instructional plans or course syllabi, ensuring that lesson goals and learning objectives are available to those who need them. It applies to teachers and school staff responsible for planning and delivering instruction across the district. For parents and community members, this policy matters because it supports transparency in the classroom, making it easier to understand what students are expected to learn and how teachers are planning to teach it.
